nodes.label: Arsenic (inorganic)
nodes.detail: Methylation-dependent metabolism; oxidative DNA damage; DNA repair inhibition via PARP1
nodes.exposure: Contaminated groundwater, rice, occupational (smelting, wood preservation)

nodes.label: Cadmium
nodes.detail: Oxidative stress; displaces zinc in DNA repair proteins; epigenetic disruption of gene expression
nodes.exposure: Tobacco smoke, Ni-Cd batteries, contaminated food (rice, shellfish)

nodes.label: Chromium (VI)
nodes.detail: Intracellular reduction to Cr(III) via ascorbate (80-90%) and GSH; forms binary and ternary Cr-DNA adducts
nodes.exposure: Stainless steel welding, chrome plating, leather tanning, pigments

nodes.label: Nickel compounds
nodes.detail: Oxidative stress; epigenetic silencing via histone modification; DNA repair inhibition (NER, BER); MGMT promoter methylation
nodes.exposure: Stainless steel, nickel refining, electroplating, occupational

nodes.label: Beryllium
nodes.detail: Immune-mediated carcinogenesis; HLA-DPB1 Glu69-restricted T cell activation; granulomatous lung disease
nodes.exposure: Aerospace, electronics, nuclear, dental alloys

nodes.label: AS3MT
nodes.detail: Arsenic (+3 oxidation state) methyltransferase; primary enzyme for iAs(III) → MMA → DMA methylation; key determinant of arsenic metabolism efficiency
nodes.variant: rs3740393, rs7085104, rs11191454 — haplotypes alter MMA/DMA ratio and cancer risk

nodes.label: GSTO1
nodes.detail: Glutathione S-transferase Omega 1; catalyzes rate-limiting reduction of MMA(V) → MMA(III) in arsenic methylation pathway
nodes.variant: Ala140Asp (rs4925) — homozygous variant + high As: OR 4.79 for urothelial carcinoma

nodes.label: GSTO2
nodes.detail: Glutathione S-transferase Omega 2; assists arsenic reduction; Asn142Asp variant associated with altered methylation capacity
nodes.variant: Asn142Asp (rs156697) — associated with increased urinary iAs%

nodes.label: MT1A
nodes.detail: Metallothionein 1A; binds and sequesters cadmium and other heavy metals; polymorphisms affect metal-binding capacity and cancer susceptibility
nodes.variant: rs11076161 AA genotype: OR 4.7 for OSCC; associated with higher Cd blood concentration and renal dysfunction

nodes.label: MT2A
nodes.detail: Metallothionein 2A; primary cadmium-binding metallothionein; zinc displacement by cadmium triggers oxidative stress and p53 disruption
nodes.variant: rs1610216, rs10636 — haplotype G/A/C/T: OR 6.2 for OSCC

nodes.label: HLA-DPB1
nodes.detail: HLA-DP Beta 1 chain; presents beryllium to CD4+ T cells; βGlu69 is primary genetic susceptibility determinant for chronic beryllium disease (CBD)
nodes.variant: βGlu69 (multiple alleles, *0201 primary) — present in 97% of CBD cases vs 30% controls; OR >30

nodes.label: PARP1
nodes.detail: Poly(ADP-ribose) polymerase 1; zinc finger protein critical for BER of 8-OHdG; INHIBITED by arsenic at concentrations as low as 1 μM via zinc displacement
nodes.variant: Functional inhibition by arsenic rather than genetic polymorphism

nodes.label: SLC11A1
nodes.detail: Solute carrier family 11A1 (NRAMP1); divalent metal ion transporter; polymorphisms affect cellular uptake of Cd, Ni, and other divalent metals
nodes.variant: Multiple SNPs associated with metal accumulation levels in GWAS (PMC2898853)

nodes.label: MMA(III)
nodes.detail: Monomethylarsonous acid; most toxic arsenic metabolite; more genotoxic than parent inorganic arsenic; generates ROS

nodes.label: MMA(V)
nodes.detail: Monomethylarsonic acid; oxidized pentavalent form; GSTO1 reduces back to MMA(III) in rate-limiting step

nodes.label: DMA(V)
nodes.detail: Dimethylarsinic acid; final methylation product; less toxic than MMA; primary urinary excretion form; higher %DMA = more efficient metabolism = lower cancer risk

nodes.label: Cr(III)
nodes.detail: Chromium(III); ultimate intracellular species from Cr(VI) reduction; binds N7-guanine in DNA major groove

nodes.label: Cr(V)
nodes.detail: Chromium(V); transient intermediate during GSH-mediated Cr(VI) reduction; potent ROS generator via Fenton-like chemistry

nodes.label: Ascorbate-Cr(III)
nodes.detail: Ascorbate-Cr(III) complex; formed during ascorbate-mediated Cr(VI) reduction (80-90% of intracellular reduction); precursor to most mutagenic Cr-DNA adduct

nodes.label: Cd-Metallothionein
nodes.detail: Cadmium sequestered by metallothionein; Cd displaces Zn from MT, disrupting Zn-dependent proteins (DNA repair enzymes, p53)

nodes.label: ROS (metal-induced)
nodes.detail: Reactive oxygen species from metal-catalyzed Fenton reactions; superoxide, hydroxyl radical, H2O2; generated by As, Cr(V), Ni, Cd

nodes.label: 8-OHdG
nodes.detail: 8-Hydroxy-2'-deoxyguanosine; major oxidative DNA damage marker; causes G:C → T:A transversions; primary lesion from arsenic and nickel exposure; repaired by OGG1/BER

nodes.label: Binary Cr(III)-DNA
nodes.detail: Cr(III) bound to N7-guanine in major groove; accounts for 75-95% of Cr-DNA bound species in cells with restored ascorbate; weakly mutagenic

nodes.label: Asc-Cr(III)-DNA
nodes.detail: Ternary ascorbate-Cr(III)-DNA crosslink; 31x more mutagenic than binary adduct; ~6% of total Cr-DNA; G/C→T/A transversions (51%), G/C→A/T transitions (30%); processed by error-prone bypass polymerases

nodes.label: Ni-DNA-Protein XL
nodes.detail: Nickel-mediated DNA-protein crosslinks; histone-DNA crosslinks from Ni binding to histone proteins; disrupts chromatin structure and gene expression

nodes.label: Heavy Metal Carcinogenesis
nodes.detail: Composite pathway integrating oxidative stress, metal homeostasis, DNA repair inhibition, and epigenetic modification mechanisms common to carcinogenic metals

nodes.label: Formaldehyde
nodes.detail: Building materials, industrial use, vehicle exhaust; also produced endogenously via one-carbon metabolism. IARC Group 1 carcinogen for nasopharyngeal cancer.
nodes.exposure: inhalation (occupational, indoor air), endogenous metabolism

nodes.label: Acetaldehyde
nodes.detail: Primary metabolite of ethanol (ADH1B oxidation); also found in tobacco smoke and fruit ripening. IARC Group 1 carcinogen associated with esophageal squamous cell carcinoma.
nodes.exposure: ethanol metabolism, tobacco smoke, fermented foods

nodes.label: TCDD
nodes.detail: 2,3,7,8-Tetrachlorodibenzo-para-dioxin; most potent AhR ligand with picomolar binding affinity. Half-life 7-11 years. Acts as tumor promoter via epigenetic reprogramming, not direct genotoxin.
nodes.exposure: industrial byproduct, incineration, contaminated food (bioaccumulation)

nodes.label: PCB 126
nodes.detail: 3,3',4,4',5-Pentachlorobiphenyl; dioxin-like PCB with WHO-TEF 0.1 relative to TCDD. Persistent organic pollutant that activates AhR pathway.
nodes.exposure: legacy industrial contamination, fish/seafood bioaccumulation

nodes.label: NDEA
nodes.detail: N-Nitrosodiethylamine; structurally related to NDMA. Found in cured meats, tobacco, and cosmetics. Activated by CYP2E1 alpha-hydroxylation analogous to NDMA pathway.
nodes.exposure: cured meats, tobacco smoke, cosmetics

nodes.label: TCE
nodes.detail: Trichloroethylene; Group 1 carcinogen for kidney cancer. Unique inverted bioactivation: GSTT1-mediated GSH conjugation is the bioactivation pathway (not detoxification), generating renal-toxic metabolites via CCBL1.
nodes.exposure: dry cleaning, metal degreasing, Superfund sites, contaminated groundwater

nodes.label: PCE
nodes.detail: Tetrachloroethylene (perchloroethylene); current dry cleaning solvent. CYP2E1 oxidation to trichloroacetic acid. IARC Group 2A probable carcinogen.
nodes.exposure: dry cleaning (occupational/residential), contaminated water

nodes.label: ALDH2
nodes.detail: Mitochondrial aldehyde dehydrogenase 2; primary acetaldehyde and formaldehyde clearance. rs671 (ALDH2*2): 6-19x acetaldehyde accumulation.

nodes.label: ADH1B
nodes.detail: Alcohol dehydrogenase 1B; oxidizes ethanol to acetaldehyde. ADH1B*2 (rs1229984) fast allele; ESCC OR 2.50 in alcohol consumers.

nodes.label: ADH1C
nodes.detail: Alcohol dehydrogenase 1C (gamma subunit); contributes to ethanol oxidation. rs698 (Ile349Val) modulates catalytic rate.

nodes.label: ADH5
nodes.detail: Formaldehyde dehydrogenase (class III ADH); cytosolic formaldehyde clearance via S-hydroxymethylglutathione pathway.

nodes.label: FANCD2
nodes.detail: Fanconi anemia complementation group D2; repairs acetaldehyde-induced DNA interstrand crosslinks (ICLs).

nodes.label: AHR
nodes.detail: Aryl hydrocarbon receptor; ligand-activated transcription factor. Binds TCDD/dioxin-like compounds and induces CYP1A1/CYP1B1 via XRE elements.

nodes.label: ARNT
nodes.detail: AhR nuclear translocator (HIF-1beta); obligate co-factor for AhR. Forms AhR-ARNT heterodimer that binds XRE/DRE elements.

nodes.label: AHRR
nodes.detail: AhR repressor; negative feedback regulator. cg05575921 CpG hypomethylation is a validated blood-based biomarker for tobacco/TCDD exposure.

nodes.label: CCBL1
nodes.detail: Cysteine conjugate beta-lyase 1; renal proximal tubule enzyme that bioactivates DCVC to reactive DCVT thiol, causing DNA adducts and VHL mutations. rs2293968/rs2280841/rs2259043/rs941960 tagging SNPs.

nodes.label: Acetaldehyde (intracellular)
nodes.detail: Intracellular acetaldehyde; reactive electrophile that forms DNA adducts and protein cross-links.

nodes.label: Formate
nodes.detail: Terminal product of formaldehyde oxidation; urinary biomarker of formaldehyde exposure.

nodes.label: AhR-ARNT complex
nodes.detail: Active transcription factor heterodimer; binds XRE/DRE elements to induce CYP1A1/CYP1B1. Not a metabolite per se but modeled as an intermediate complex.

nodes.label: Hydroxymethylnitrosamine
nodes.detail: Alpha-hydroxylation product of NDMA/NDEA; proximate carcinogen that spontaneously decomposes to methyldiazonium + formaldehyde.

nodes.label: Methyldiazonium ion
nodes.detail: Ultimate reactive alkylating species produced by nitrosamine alpha-hydroxylation. Methylates guanine O6 to form the primary mutagenic lesion for the NDMA branch.

nodes.label: DCVG
nodes.detail: S-(1,2-dichlorovinyl)glutathione; GSTT1 conjugation product of TCE. Transported to kidney for further processing.

nodes.label: DCVC
nodes.detail: S-(1,2-dichlorovinyl)-L-cysteine; renal processing product of DCVG. Substrate for CCBL1 beta-lyase bioactivation.

nodes.label: Chloral hydrate
nodes.detail: CYP2E1 oxidative metabolite of TCE; hepatotoxic intermediate oxidized to trichloroacetic acid.

nodes.label: TCA
nodes.detail: Trichloroacetic acid; stable urinary biomarker for TCE/PCE exposure.

nodes.label: N2-HOMedG
nodes.detail: N2-hydroxymethyl-dG; formaldehyde-DNA adduct. ~20-fold increase in ALDH2/ADH5 double-knockout mice.

nodes.label: N2-ethylidene-dG
nodes.detail: Acetaldehyde-DNA adduct; forms interstrand crosslinks (ICLs) requiring Fanconi anemia pathway repair.

nodes.label: Renal DNA damage
nodes.detail: DCVC-derived DCVT thiol forms DNA adducts in renal proximal tubule; associated with VHL tumor suppressor mutations in renal cell carcinoma.

nodes.label: Aldehyde Carcinogenesis
nodes.detail: Composite pathway covering formaldehyde and acetaldehyde metabolism, DNA adduct formation, and Fanconi anemia repair.

nodes.label: AhR-Mediated Transcriptional Induction
nodes.detail: AhR/ARNT-mediated transcriptional induction of CYP1A1/CYP1B1; tumor promotion via epigenetic reprogramming without direct DNA damage.

nodes.label: N-Nitroso Compound Activation
nodes.detail: CYP2E1/CYP2A6-mediated alpha-hydroxylation pathway for dietary nitrosamines; generates alkylating methyldiazonium ions.

nodes.label: Chlorinated Solvent Dual Pathway
nodes.detail: Dual metabolic pathway: CYP2E1 oxidative (hepatic) and GSTT1 GSH conjugation bioactivation (renal). The GSH pathway is uniquely a bioactivation route.

nodes.label: Ethanol
nodes.detail: Upstream alcohol substrate represented to connect ADH-mediated oxidation to intracellular acetaldehyde formation. Included as a package bridge so the aldehyde pathway resolves in standalone and merged graphs.
nodes.exposure: alcoholic beverages

nodes.label: N2,3-ethenoguanine
nodes.detail: Representative etheno adduct associated with vinyl chloride exposure.
nodes.evidence: Representative etheno adduct associated with vinyl chloride exposure.

provenance.evidence: Representative etheno adduct associated with vinyl chloride exposure.
provenance.citation: Curated DNA adduct entry for N2,3-ethenoguanine

nodes.evidence_id: Curated DNA adduct entry for N2,3-ethenoguanine

nodes.label: Acrylamide
nodes.detail: Water-soluble vinyl monomer generated by Maillard chemistry in high-temperature cooking of starchy foods (fried/baked potatoes, coffee roasting) and by polyacrylamide manufacture. Bioactivated by CYP2E1 epoxidation to glycidamide, the ultimate mutagen. References: IARC Monograph Vol 60 (1994); Besaratinia & Pfeifer, Carcinogenesis 2007 (PMID:17872912).
nodes.exposure: high-temperature cooked starchy foods, tobacco smoke, polyacrylamide industry

nodes.label: Glycidamide
nodes.detail: 2,3-Epoxypropanamide; direct-acting epoxide and ultimate mutagenic metabolite of acrylamide. Forms N7-(2-carbamoyl-2-hydroxyethyl)-dG and N3-GA-dA DNA adducts. Detoxified by GSTP1/GSTM1 glutathione conjugation. References: Segerbaeck et al., Carcinogenesis 1995 (PMID:7614537); Besaratinia, Mutat Res 2010.
nodes.exposure: endogenous formation from acrylamide via CYP2E1

nodes.label: Cyclophosphamide
nodes.detail: Oxazaphosphorine prodrug chemotherapeutic. Bioactivated primarily by CYP2B6 (major) and CYP3A4/2C9 (minor) to 4-hydroxycyclophosphamide, which ring-opens to aldophosphamide and fragments to phosphoramide mustard (ultimate DNA-crosslinking agent) plus acrolein. Associated with secondary bladder cancer and therapy-related AML. References: Roy et al., Drug Metab Dispos 1999 (PMID:10220571); IARC Monograph Vol 100A (2012).
nodes.exposure: therapeutic chemotherapy (lymphomas, breast cancer, autoimmune disease)

nodes.label: Chlorambucil
nodes.detail: Aromatic nitrogen mustard. Forms aziridinium ion via intramolecular cyclization, yielding N7-guanine monoadducts and G-G interstrand crosslinks. Detoxified by GSTP1 glutathione conjugation; GSTP1 Ile105Val (rs1695) modulates treatment response and therapy-related leukemia risk. References: Povirk & Shuker, Mutat Res 1994 (PMID:7523912); IARC Monograph Vol 100A (2012).
nodes.exposure: therapeutic chemotherapy (CLL, Waldenstroem macroglobulinemia, ovarian cancer)

nodes.label: Sulfur mustard
nodes.detail: Bis(2-chloroethyl) sulfide. Chemical-warfare vesicant that cyclizes to a reactive episulfonium ion, generating N7-mustard-Gua monoadducts and 5'-d(GNC) interstrand crosslinks. Linked to lung cancer in exposed workers and veterans. References: Matijasevic et al., Carcinogenesis 1996 (PMID:8635461); IARC Monograph Vol 100F (2012).
nodes.exposure: chemical-weapon exposure, legacy ordnance, historic occupational mustard-gas factories

nodes.label: Busulfan
nodes.detail: 1,4-Butanediol dimethanesulfonate; bifunctional alkyl sulfonate used in HSCT conditioning. Hydrolyzes non-enzymatically to a reactive methanesulfonate intermediate, forming N7-(2,3,4-trihydroxybutyl)-dG adducts and DNA-DNA interstrand crosslinks. Detoxified by GSTA1/GSTP1 glutathione conjugation (GSH-Bu). References: Hassan & Ljungman, Mutat Res 1997 (PMID:12960109); IARC Monograph Vol 100A (2012).
nodes.exposure: therapeutic chemotherapy (CML, bone-marrow-transplant conditioning)

nodes.label: MNU (N-methyl-N-nitrosourea)
nodes.detail: N-methyl-N-nitrosourea; direct-acting methylating agent requiring no enzymatic activation. Spontaneously decomposes at physiological pH to methyldiazohydroxide/methyldiazonium, alkylating DNA at O6-guanine (G:C to A:T transitions) and N7-guanine. Canonical model carcinogen for MGMT biology. References: Kyrtopoulos, Mutat Res 1990 (PMID:2185966); IARC Monograph Vol 17 (1978).
nodes.exposure: experimental/research reagent; trace formation in cured meats

nodes.label: Temozolomide
nodes.detail: Imidazotetrazine prodrug. Spontaneous pH-dependent hydrolysis yields MTIC (5-(3-methyltriazen-1-yl)imidazole-4-carboxamide), which decomposes to methyldiazonium ion. Alkylates O6-guanine; therapeutic efficacy in glioblastoma tracks MGMT promoter-methylation status. References: Newlands et al., Cancer Treat Rev 1997 (PMID:9327140); Hegi et al., NEJM 2005 (PMID:15758009).
nodes.exposure: therapeutic chemotherapy (glioblastoma, metastatic melanoma)

nodes.label: 4-Hydroxycyclophosphamide
nodes.detail: Primary CYP2B6/CYP3A4 activation product of cyclophosphamide; equilibrates with aldophosphamide and fragments to phosphoramide mustard plus acrolein (PMID:10220571).

nodes.label: Phosphoramide mustard
nodes.detail: Ultimate DNA-crosslinking species from cyclophosphamide activation; bifunctional nitrogen mustard that forms G-G interstrand crosslinks via aziridinium intermediates (PMID:10220571).

nodes.label: Chlorambucil aziridinium
nodes.detail: Cyclic aziridinium cation formed by intramolecular cyclization of chlorambucil; alkylates N7-guanine and generates interstrand crosslinks (PMID:7523912).

nodes.label: Sulfur-mustard episulfonium ion
nodes.detail: Cyclic sulfonium intermediate formed by intramolecular cyclization of sulfur mustard; ultimate electrophile for N7-Gua monoadducts and 5'-d(GNC) interstrand crosslinks (PMID:8635461).

nodes.label: Busulfan methanesulfonate intermediate
nodes.detail: Reactive bifunctional alkylator generated by hydrolytic displacement of methanesulfonate groups from busulfan; yields N7-THPG adducts and DNA-DNA interstrand crosslinks (PMID:12960109).

nodes.label: MTIC
nodes.detail: 5-(3-methyltriazen-1-yl)imidazole-4-carboxamide; monomethyltriazene formed by spontaneous hydrolysis of temozolomide, which in turn decomposes to methyldiazonium (PMID:9327140).

nodes.label: N7-GA-dG
nodes.detail: N7-(2-carbamoyl-2-hydroxyethyl)-2'-deoxyguanosine; dominant glycidamide-DNA adduct from acrylamide exposure. Depurinates to abasic site repaired by BER (PMID:17872912).

nodes.label: N7-methyl-dG
nodes.detail: N7-methyl-2'-deoxyguanosine; most abundant methylation lesion from MNU and temozolomide via methyldiazonium. Non-mutagenic itself but labile; depurinates to abasic sites repaired by BER (XRCC1 scaffold).

nodes.label: G-G interstrand crosslink (mustard-type)
nodes.detail: Bifunctional N7-alkyl-dG interstrand crosslink generated by phosphoramide mustard (cyclophosphamide), chlorambucil aziridinium, sulfur-mustard episulfonium, and the busulfan methanesulfonate intermediate. Requires Fanconi-anemia/HR repair; mechanistic basis for therapeutic cytotoxicity (PMID:10220571, PMID:7523912, PMID:8635461, PMID:12960109).

nodes.label: Alkylating Agent Carcinogenesis
nodes.detail: Composite pathway covering direct-acting methylators (MNU), prodrug-activated alkylators (cyclophosphamide, temozolomide), chemical-warfare mustards (sulfur mustard), bifunctional sulfonates (busulfan), and CYP2E1-activated acrylamide/glycidamide converging on N7/O6-guanine adducts and DNA interstrand crosslinks.

nodes.label: Urethane (ethyl carbamate)
nodes.detail: Ethyl carbamate; natural contaminant of fermented foods and alcoholic beverages, especially stone-fruit brandies, sake, sherry, and bread crust. CYP2E1 dehydrogenates urethane to vinyl carbamate, which is then re-epoxidized to vinyl carbamate epoxide — the ultimate mutagen that forms etheno-A/etheno-C DNA adducts (same adduct family as vinyl chloride). References: Hoffler et al., Toxicology 2003 (PMID:12860588); IARC Monograph Vol 96 (2010).
nodes.exposure: alcoholic beverages (stone-fruit brandies, sake, sherry), fermented foods, bread crust

nodes.label: Acrolein
nodes.detail: 2-Propenal; smallest alpha,beta-unsaturated aldehyde and potent Michael acceptor. Major constituents of cigarette smoke, heated cooking oils, and automobile exhaust; also generated in vivo as the obligate byproduct of cyclophosphamide activation (4-hydroxycyclophosphamide fragmentation). Forms alpha/gamma-OH-1,N2-propano-dG (Acr-dG) exocyclic adducts, which are mispairing, bulky lesions repaired by NER/BER. Detoxified by GSTP1 (GS-HPMA) and ALDH2 oxidation to acrylic acid. References: IARC Monograph Vol 128 (2021); Minko et al., Chem Res Toxicol 2009 (PMID:19705912).
nodes.exposure: tobacco smoke, heated cooking oils, wildfire/vehicle exhaust, cyclophosphamide metabolism

nodes.label: Crotonaldehyde
nodes.detail: (E)-2-Butenal; alpha,beta-unsaturated aldehyde abundant in tobacco smoke, alcoholic beverages (congener), and heated cooking oils. Forms cyclic 1,N2-propano-dG (Cr-dG) exocyclic adducts via Michael addition/cyclization; adducts are miscoding and persist in liver, oral mucosa, and lung tissue. References: Chung et al., Carcinogenesis 1989; IARC Monograph Vol 63 (1995).
nodes.exposure: tobacco smoke, alcoholic beverages, heated cooking oils, air pollutant

nodes.label: Furfural
nodes.detail: Furan-2-carbaldehyde; Maillard-reaction aldehyde abundant in roasted coffee, beer, bread, and heated fruit products. Bioactivated via sequential reduction/hydroxylation to 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF) then sulfated by SULT1A1 to 5-sulfoxymethylfurfural (SMF), a DNA-reactive electrophile that alkylates dA/dG. Classified IARC Group 3 but included for mechanistic completeness of dietary aldehyde exposures. References: Monien et al., Mol Nutr Food Res 2011 (PMID:21538843); EFSA CONTAM Panel Opinion 2011.
nodes.exposure: roasted coffee, beer, bread, heated fruit/sugar products

nodes.label: Malondialdehyde (MDA)
nodes.detail: Propanedial; ubiquitous lipid-peroxidation end-product and a direct-acting mutagen. Universal biomarker of oxidative stress. Reacts with dG to form pyrimido[1,2-a]purin-10(3H)-one (M1-dG), a miscoding adduct that causes base substitutions and frameshifts. Elevated in chronic inflammation, alcoholic liver disease, and hepatocarcinogenesis. References: Marnett, Mutat Res 1999 (PMID:10064842); Niedernhofer et al., J Biol Chem 2003.
nodes.exposure: endogenous lipid peroxidation, oxidative stress, chronic inflammation

nodes.label: 4-Hydroxynonenal (4-HNE)
nodes.detail: (E)-4-Hydroxy-2-nonenal; the most reactive major product of omega-6 polyunsaturated fatty-acid peroxidation. Bifunctional Michael acceptor that forms exocyclic 1,N2-propano-dG (HNE-dG) adducts, preferentially at methylated CpG sites (a molecular link between oxidative stress and TP53 hotspot mutations). Detoxified by GSTP1 glutathione conjugation and ALDH2 oxidation. References: Esterbauer, Schaur & Zollner, Free Radic Biol Med 1991 (PMID:1937131); Hu et al., PNAS 2002.
nodes.exposure: endogenous omega-6 lipid peroxidation, oxidative stress, alcoholic steatohepatitis

nodes.label: Vinyl carbamate
nodes.detail: CYP2E1-derived dehydrogenation product of urethane; proximal intermediate re-epoxidized to vinyl carbamate epoxide (PMID:12860588).

nodes.label: Vinyl carbamate epoxide
nodes.detail: Ultimate mutagenic metabolite of urethane; alkylates dA and dC to yield etheno-DNA adducts equivalent to those formed by vinyl chloride (PMID:12860588).

nodes.label: 5-sulfoxymethylfurfural (SMF)
nodes.detail: SULT1A1-derived sulfate ester of 5-hydroxymethylfurfural (HMF); DNA-reactive electrophile that alkylates dA/dG. HMF itself arises from hydroxylation of furfural in heated foods (PMID:21538843).

nodes.label: alpha/gamma-OH-PdG (Acr-dG)
nodes.detail: alpha- and gamma-hydroxy-1,N2-propano-2'-deoxyguanosine; major Michael-addition adduct from acrolein reacting at N1/N2 of guanine. Miscoding in ring-opened form; repaired by NER with XRCC1/BER backup (PMID:19705912).

nodes.label: Cr-1,N2-PdG (Cr-dG)
nodes.detail: Cyclic 1,N2-propano-dG adduct from crotonaldehyde Michael addition; miscoding adduct detected in liver, oral mucosa, and lung of smokers (IARC Monograph Vol 63; Chung et al. 1989).

nodes.label: M1-dG (pyrimido-purinone)
nodes.detail: 3-(2'-deoxy-beta-D-erythro-pentofuranosyl)pyrimido[1,2-a]purin-10(3H)-one; principal MDA-DNA adduct. Miscoding lesion causing base substitutions and frameshifts; basal levels in healthy tissues provide a baseline for oxidative-stress-driven mutagenesis (PMID:10064842).

nodes.label: HNE-1,N2-PdG (HNE-dG)
nodes.detail: Exocyclic 1,N2-propano-dG adduct from 4-HNE Michael addition/cyclization; formation is strongly enhanced at methylated CpG sites, mechanistically linking lipid peroxidation to TP53 hotspot mutations (Hu et al., PNAS 2002; PMID:1937131).

nodes.label: PCB 169
nodes.detail: 3,3',4,4',5,5'-Hexachlorobiphenyl; non-ortho dioxin-like PCB with WHO-TEF 0.03. Potent AhR agonist contributing to total toxic equivalency (TEQ) in serum/food (IARC Monograph 107).
nodes.exposure: legacy transformer/capacitor oils, fatty fish, dairy, breast milk

nodes.label: PCB 77
nodes.detail: 3,3',4,4'-Tetrachlorobiphenyl; coplanar dioxin-like PCB with WHO-TEF 0.0001. Short half-life relative to higher-chlorinated PCBs but remains a marker of recent exposure (IARC Monograph 107).
nodes.exposure: combustion byproduct, contaminated sediments, dietary

nodes.label: PCB 118
nodes.detail: 2,3',4,4',5-Pentachlorobiphenyl; mono-ortho dioxin-like PCB (WHO-TEF 0.00003). Most abundant dioxin-like PCB congener in human serum; used as a biomarker of cumulative PCB exposure (IARC Monograph 107).
nodes.exposure: fish consumption, occupational exposure in electrical industry, background dietary

nodes.label: PCB 153
nodes.detail: 2,2',4,4',5,5'-Hexachlorobiphenyl; non-dioxin-like (NDL) PCB, most abundant PCB in human tissues. Activates CAR/PXR rather than AhR; tumor promoter via oxidative stress and gap-junction inhibition (IARC Monograph 107).
nodes.exposure: fatty fish, meat, dairy; breast milk transfer; highly persistent

nodes.label: PCB 138
nodes.detail: 2,2',3,4,4',5'-Hexachlorobiphenyl; non-dioxin-like PCB co-dominant with PCB-153 in human serum. CAR/PXR activator; commonly reported in NHANES biomonitoring panels (IARC Monograph 107).
nodes.exposure: bioaccumulative dietary exposure, adipose storage, transplacental transfer

nodes.label: 2,3,4,7,8-PeCDF
nodes.detail: 2,3,4,7,8-Pentachlorodibenzofuran; most potent non-TCDD AhR ligand with WHO-TEF 0.3. Major contributor to Yusho/Yu-cheng poisoning TEQ and dominant PCDF in human adipose (IARC Monograph 100F).
nodes.exposure: PCB combustion, rice-oil contamination (Yusho/Yu-cheng), incineration

nodes.label: Hexachlorobenzene (HCB)
nodes.detail: Persistent organochlorine fungicide and industrial byproduct; weak AhR agonist and CYP1A1/CYP1B1 inducer. Linked to hepatic porphyria, thyroid cancer, and elevated breast-cancer risk (IARC Monograph 79).
nodes.exposure: legacy fungicide residues, chlorinated solvent manufacture, dietary fat

nodes.label: Lindane (γ-HCH)
nodes.detail: Gamma-hexachlorocyclohexane; organochlorine insecticide classified Group 1 by IARC in 2015 (Monograph 113) based on non-Hodgkin lymphoma evidence. Acts via CAR/PXR-mediated CYP2B/CYP3A induction, GABA-A receptor antagonism, and oxidative stress.
nodes.exposure: legacy agricultural insecticide, head-lice pharmaceutical, persistent in soil and food chain

nodes.label: DDT
nodes.detail: Dichlorodiphenyltrichloroethane; classified Group 2A by IARC in 2015 (Monograph 113) based on liver, testicular, and non-Hodgkin lymphoma evidence. Primarily CAR/PXR-mediated tumor promoter; weak AhR activity. Dechlorinated to DDE via CYP2B-mediated metabolism.
nodes.exposure: legacy agricultural/vector-control insecticide, global body-burden biomarker

nodes.label: p,p'-DDE
nodes.detail: Principal DDT metabolite and dominant organochlorine in human adipose tissue. Antiandrogenic AR antagonist and CAR/PXR activator; persistent half-life of 10+ years. Biomarker of cumulative DDT exposure (IARC Monograph 113, 2015).
nodes.exposure: bioaccumulation from DDT residues, fatty fish, dairy, adipose storage, transplacental transfer

nodes.label: Pentachlorophenol (PCP)
nodes.detail: Chlorinated phenol wood preservative and biocide; classified Group 1 by IARC in 2019 (Monograph 117) based on non-Hodgkin lymphoma and multiple myeloma evidence. AhR agonist with redox-cycling tetrachloro-p-benzoquinone metabolite; often co-contaminated with dioxins/furans.
nodes.exposure: sawmill/lumber workers, treated wood products, contaminated soil and groundwater

nodes.label: Chlordane (technical)
nodes.detail: Technical-grade cyclodiene insecticide mixture; classified Group 2B by IARC in 1991 (Monograph 53). Primarily CAR/PXR activator; liver tumor promoter in rodents; persists in indoor dust from historic termiticide applications.
nodes.exposure: legacy subterranean termiticide, indoor dust residues, dietary bioaccumulation

nodes.label: Heptachlor
nodes.detail: Cyclodiene insecticide co-formulated with chlordane; classified Group 2B by IARC in 1991 (Monograph 53). Bioactivated to more toxic heptachlor epoxide by hepatic CYPs; CAR/PXR-mediated tumor promotion.
nodes.exposure: legacy termiticide, soil/sediment residues, dietary exposure via meat and dairy

nodes.label: Toxaphene
nodes.detail: Complex chlorinated camphene mixture (~670 congeners); classified Group 2B by IARC in 2001 (Monograph 79). Mixed CAR/PXR and weak AhR agonism; GABA-A antagonism; long-range atmospheric transport to Arctic food webs.
nodes.exposure: post-DDT-ban cotton insecticide, persistent in fish/marine mammals, Arctic bioaccumulation
